articles.title: People warned of rising heatwave conditions - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
People warned of rising heatwave conditions
Vijayawada: People in Andhra Pradesh have been advised to take precautions against rising temperatures, heatwave conditions and increasing humidity across the state, according to Prakhar Jain.
The official said temperatures are gradually increasing across several districts. On Wednesday, the highest temperature of 43.7 degrees Celsius was recorded at Rayalapeta in Chittoor district. Temperatures above 40°C were reported in 143 mandals spread across 22 of the State’s 28 districts. Jain said that maximum temperatures between 43°C and 44°C are likely to be recorded on Thursday. Heatwave conditions are expected in four mandals including G.K. Veedhi, Chintoor, Kunavaram and Gurtedu. On Friday, severe heatwave conditions may prevail in one mandal, while heatwave conditions are likely in 15 mandals.
At the same time, due to the influence of a low-pressure system, scattered thunderstorms accompanied by lightning are likely in parts of Rayalaseema over the next two days. Other major temperatures recorded on Wednesday include 42.6°C at Dornipadu, 42.5°C at Nagaradon, 42°C at Kalasapadu and 41.8°C at Vuyyuru.
People were advised to avoid going outdoors during peak heat hours and protect themselves from hot winds. Those suffering from heart ailments, diabetes and blood pressure problems were urged to avoid exposure to the sun. Officials also recommended drinking plenty of water, buttermilk, lemon juice and coconut water to stay hydrated.
